name: FAL Avatar (Talking Head)
description: Create persistent avatars and lip-synced talking-head videos (HeyGen-style) with FAL.ai: Nano Banana 2 portraits, ByteDance OmniHuman v1.5 / InfiniTalk speech animation, and Sync Lipsync 2.0 re-syncing. Includes a persistent avatar library.

required_variables.description: FAL.ai API key (get from https://fal.ai/dashboard/keys). Falls back to FAL_KEY if set.

connector.name: FAL.ai
connector.description: FAL.ai media generation API (images, video, lip-sync).

skill.name: fal_avatar
skill.description: Avatar creation + lip-synced talking-head video generation via FAL.ai (OmniHuman, InfiniTalk, Sync Lipsync, Nano Banana 2) with a persistent avatar library.
skill.system_prompt:
You can create persistent avatars and make them speak in lip-synced videos using FAL.ai.

## Workflow (HeyGen-style avatar video)
1. Get the avatar: `list_avatars` to reuse an existing one by name, or `create_avatar` to make a new portrait (pass `reference_image` to turn a user's photo into an avatar; use `save_as` to store it in the library).
2. Get the speech audio: use your TTS tool (`generate_speech` from ElevenLabs skill for cloned voices, or `generate_voiceover` from OpenAI TTS) to produce an mp3 in the workspace.
3. Animate: `talking_video` (OmniHuman v1.5 — best quality; 60s max at 720p, 30s at 1080p) or `talking_video_budget` (InfiniTalk — cheaper drafts).
4. Optional post: compose captions/music with the video-composer skill or ffmpeg.

## Rules
- Avatar portraits work best head-and-shoulders, facing camera, neutral background, mouth closed, single subject.
- Lip-sync models are billed per second of audio/video. Keep speech tight; do not generate minutes of video without being asked.
- Split long scripts into <60s chunks, generate one talking_video per chunk, then concatenate with ffmpeg (`stitch_clips` pattern or concat demuxer).
- Always save reusable characters with `save_avatar` (or `create_avatar save_as=...`) — the library persists across tasks; workspace files do not.
- Always include the final `video_file` path in your task result so it can be delivered to the user.

tools.name: create_avatar
tools.description: Create an avatar portrait image with FAL Nano Banana 2 (Gemini 3 Pro Image). Text-to-image by default; pass reference_image (path or URL, e.g. the user's photo) to transform a real photo into an avatar while keeping the person's identity. Optionally saves straight into the persistent avatar library with save_as. Returns JSON {image_file, image_url, saved:{name, file}}.

prompt.description: Portrait description (character, style, expression, background). For talking-head videos ask for: head-and-shoulders framing, facing camera, neutral background, mouth closed.

reference_image.description: Optional photo to base the avatar on: local file path, URL, or library avatar name. Uses the image-edit endpoint so identity is preserved.

save_as.description: Optional avatar name. When set, the image is copied into the persistent avatar library so it can be reused across tasks by name.

description.description: Optional library description stored alongside the avatar (only used with save_as).

tools.name: talking_video
tools.description: Make an avatar SPEAK: generate a lip-synced talking-head video from an avatar image + an audio file using ByteDance OmniHuman v1.5 on FAL (best quality). Audio limits: 60s at 720p, 30s at 1080p. Billed per second of audio — keep clips short. Returns JSON {video_file, video_url, duration}.

avatar.description: Avatar image: library name (see list_avatars), local file path, or URL.

audio.description: Speech audio: local file path (e.g. output of generate_speech / generate_voiceover) or URL. Max 60s (720p) / 30s (1080p).

prompt.description: Optional guidance for motion/emotion (e.g. 'speaking enthusiastically to the camera, subtle hand gestures').

turbo.description: Faster generation with slight quality trade-off. Default false.

tools.name: talking_video_budget
tools.description: Budget alternative to talking_video: lip-synced talking avatar via FAL InfiniTalk (~$0.2/s, 480p/720p, lower fidelity than OmniHuman). Good for drafts and iterations. Returns JSON {video_file, video_url}.

avatar.description: Avatar image: library name, local file path, or URL.

audio.description: Speech audio: local file path or URL.

prompt.description: Scene description, e.g. 'A woman talking on a podcast'. Required by the model.

num_frames.description: 41-721 frames (~25fps). Default 145. Should roughly cover the audio length.

tools.name: lipsync_video
tools.description: Re-lipsync an EXISTING video to new audio using Sync Lipsync 2.0 on FAL ($3/min). Use when you already have avatar footage (e.g. from image_to_video) and want it to speak different audio. Returns JSON {video_file, video_url}.

video.description: Input video: local file path or URL.

audio.description: New speech audio: local file path or URL.

tools.name: save_avatar
tools.description: Save an image into the persistent avatar library (survives task cleanup) so it can be reused by name in talking_video across tasks. Returns the manifest JSON.

source.description: Image to save: local file path or URL.

name.description: Avatar name (library key, e.g. 'ray'). Lowercased/slugged for the file name.

tools.name: list_avatars
tools.description: List avatars in the persistent library (name, file path, description). Use the returned names with talking_video / create_avatar reference_image.
